ogTitle: Atkinsons Dam vs Auburn: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Atkinsons Dam and Auburn.
heading: Atkinsons Dam vs Auburn — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Atkinsons Dam, Queensland and Auburn, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Auburn has a lower overall climate risk score (32/100) compared to Atkinsons Dam (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Atkinsons Dam or Auburn safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Atkinsons Dam has a flood risk score of 23/100, while Auburn scores 39/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Atkinsons Dam scores 15/100 for bushfire risk. Auburn scores 38/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Atkinsons Dam or Auburn?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Auburn has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.62% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
